text.title: Prompt Engineering — Security and Injection Defense — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-engineering-security methodology. Sources: Greshake et al. — Prompt-injection survey (arXiv:2302.12173); OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications; Anthropic constitutional AI + injection defences.

rule.statement: User-supplied text MUST be wrapped in explicit delimiters in every LLM call. For Claude, use XML tags (user_input). For GPT-4, use markdown fenced sections or labeled headers. The system prompt MUST explicitly state that instructions within user_input tags do not override system instructions.
rule.rationale: Without delimiters, the model cannot distinguish instructions from data. XML tags signal a semantic boundary that well-trained models respect, reducing (not eliminating) injection success rate.

rule.statement: Input sanitization MUST: (1) truncate inputs exceeding the maximum allowed length, (2) filter known injection pattern strings, (3) log warnings for filtered inputs. Sanitization MUST NOT be the sole defense — it MUST be combined with delimiter isolation.
rule.rationale: Pattern-based filters catch known injection strings but are bypassable via encoding or paraphrasing. They are valuable as a signal (log warnings) and as friction, not as a complete barrier.

rule.statement: System prompts for user-facing applications MUST include: (1) explicit scope definition, (2) instruction to ignore override attempts from user input, (3) a default fallback response for out-of-scope or suspicious requests. System prompts MUST NOT be revealed to users even if asked.
rule.rationale: Explicit constraints in the system prompt reduce the model's tendency to comply with injection attempts. Fallback responses prevent the model from freezing or hallucinating when confused by adversarial input.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r4).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `spec` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
